questions.q: In the Ramayana, Jambavan is known as the king of which animals?
questions.explanation: Jambavan is celebrated as the ancient and wise lord of the bears.

questions.q: Which quality is most often attributed to Jambavan in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Jambavan is depicted as an ancient and wise counsellor among the forest hosts.

questions.q: Jambavan reminded which hero of his own forgotten powers?
questions.explanation: Jambavan reminded Hanuman of his immense strength so he could leap across the ocean to Lanka.

questions.q: Jambavan was a member of which great effort organized to find Sita?
questions.explanation: Jambavan counselled the search party that was dispatched to locate the captured Sita.

questions.q: Jambavan and the search party allied themselves with which prince to recover Sita?
questions.explanation: The bears and monkeys joined Rama's cause to find and rescue Sita from Ravana.

questions.q: Jambavan belongs to the forest hosts that aided Rama. Who led the monkey kingdom they served alongside?
questions.explanation: The bears under Jambavan fought alongside the monkey army led by King Sugriva of Kishkindha.

questions.q: What role did Jambavan mainly play for the search party?
questions.explanation: Jambavan served as a wise counsellor who guided and encouraged the search party.

questions.q: Against which demon-king's forces did Jambavan and his allies ultimately fight?
questions.explanation: Jambavan and the army of bears and monkeys fought against Ravana, the king of Lanka.

questions.q: Which hero, encouraged by Jambavan, leapt across the ocean to Lanka?
questions.explanation: After Jambavan reminded him of his powers, Hanuman leapt the ocean to reach Lanka.

questions.q: Jambavan is generally portrayed as which of the following in age?
questions.explanation: Jambavan is described as an ancient being, the venerable elder of the bears.

questions.q: When the southern search party reached the ocean and despaired, what did Jambavan do?
options: He inspired Hanuman to recall his strength
questions.explanation: Jambavan recounted Hanuman's divine birth and powers, rousing him to attempt the ocean leap.

questions.q: Jambavan was part of which directional search group sent by Sugriva?
questions.explanation: Jambavan accompanied the southern search party that learned Sita was held in Lanka.

questions.q: Which young prince led the southern search party that included Jambavan and Hanuman?
questions.explanation: Angada, son of Vali, led the southern search party that included Jambavan and Hanuman.

questions.q: Whose words first revealed to the search party that Sita had been taken to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Sampati, brother of Jatayu, told the southern party that Ravana held Sita in Lanka across the sea.

questions.q: By tradition, Jambavan's origin is linked to which creator deity?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Jambavan sprang from the body of Brahma, making him exceedingly ancient.

questions.q: When Lakshmana lay wounded, Jambavan advised sending Hanuman to fetch what remedy?
questions.explanation: Jambavan counselled that Hanuman bring life-restoring herbs from the Himalayan mountain to heal the wounded.

questions.q: What does Jambavan's role as counsellor chiefly contribute to the war effort?
questions.explanation: Jambavan's chief contribution is his strategic wisdom and his ability to inspire others to their full power.

questions.q: Jambavan reminded Hanuman of his strength because of a curse Hanuman had received in childhood. What had that curse caused?
questions.explanation: A sage's curse had made Hanuman forget his powers until reminded, which Jambavan did at the ocean shore.

questions.q: In the host that aids Rama, Jambavan's bears (riksha) fight alongside which other forest creatures?
questions.explanation: Jambavan's bears fought side by side with the vanara (monkey) army in Rama's host.

questions.q: Which of these best describes Jambavan's standing among the forest leaders?
options: A senior elder whose counsel is heeded
questions.explanation: Jambavan is a senior, respected elder whose counsel the leaders of the host heed.

questions.q: Jambavan encouraged Hanuman at the shore of which body of water before the leap to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Jambavan inspired Hanuman at the shore of the great ocean separating the mainland from Lanka.

questions.q: What was the immediate goal of the search party that Jambavan counselled?
questions.explanation: The search party's immediate goal was to discover where the abducted Sita was being held.

questions.q: According to widely accepted tradition, by what measure of Hanuman's leaping ability did Jambavan gauge that Hanuman could cross the ocean?
options: By Hanuman's divine birth and past feats
questions.explanation: Jambavan recounted Hanuman's divine parentage and childhood feats to prove he could leap the hundred-yojana sea.

questions.q: In the Yuddha Kanda, Jambavan plays a key role by directing Hanuman to retrieve which specific life-saving herbs?
options: The Sanjivani and allied herbs of Mount Dronagiri
questions.explanation: Jambavan sent Hanuman to fetch the Sanjivani and other healing herbs from the Himalayan mountain to revive the fallen.

questions.q: Tradition holds that Jambavan witnessed an earlier avatar of Vishnu. Which avatar measured the cosmos in three strides during Jambavan's long life?
questions.explanation: Jambavan is said to be so ancient that he saw Vamana take the three world-spanning strides as Trivikrama.

questions.q: In Valmiki's account, after Sampati's revelation, which leaders most encouraged Hanuman to leap, with Jambavan foremost among the counsellors?
questions.explanation: Jambavan, supported by the prince Angada, chiefly urged and reassured Hanuman to undertake the ocean leap.

questions.q: Which term from the Ramayana most precisely names the kind of creature Jambavan leads?
questions.explanation: Jambavan is the lord of the rikshas (bears), a host distinct from the vanaras (monkeys).

questions.q: When the search party feared they would die having failed their mission, what did Jambavan's intervention chiefly prevent?
options: Their despairing decision to give up
questions.explanation: Jambavan's encouragement of Hanuman turned the party from hopeless despair toward success in reaching Lanka.

questions.q: Jambavan's reminder unlocked Hanuman's potential chiefly so that Hanuman could accomplish which feat first?
options: Leaping the ocean to find Sita
questions.explanation: Jambavan's reminder enabled Hanuman's first great feat: leaping the ocean to discover Sita in Lanka.

questions.q: Which statement best captures Jambavan's lasting significance in the Ramayana narrative?
options: He is a minor enemy of Rama
He is the brother of Ravana
He is the charioteer of Rama
He is the wise elder whose counsel turns the search and war in Rama's favor
questions.explanation: Jambavan endures as the venerable, wise elder whose counsel repeatedly proves decisive for Rama's cause.
